mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth in the labor market signals robust corporate earnings potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved investor sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth in 3-year rolling windows (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through heightened recession risk.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive despite UNRATE exceeding 5.0% in 60%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a contraction in real economic activity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through reduced corporate demand.
failure_condition: Interaction between INDPRO and HOUST shows no significant impact on SPX returns (p > 0.10) in 5-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real private domestic demand (RSAFS) during a recessionary regime signals weak consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show no significant negative correlation with RSAFS declines (Rank IC > -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving corporate capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO increases (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling real money balances (RSAFS) creates monetary disequilibrium, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between CPIAUCSL and RSAFS shows no significant impact on SPX returns (p > 0.10) in 5-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) during an expansionary regime signals late-cycle weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show no significant negative correlation with GDPC1 declines (Rank IC > -0.03).
